The '''Felinians''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Feline/Legends|felinoids]] originating from the [[Felin system/Legends|Felin system]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Felinians had developed technology considered primitive in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large, but a group of them residing in [[Tree/Legends|jungles]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zoma V]] desired advanced weapons such as [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], commodities for which they traded precious [[Stone/Legends|stones]]. The [[Saurian (species)|Saurian]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sithless Leethe]] attempted to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggle]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] weapons to the Felinians under the oversight of his associate, a Saurian named [[Slopper]]. Nevertheless, the [[Courier/Legends|couriers]] he had chosen to deliver the cargo—[[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to whom he had sold a [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] on condition of delivery of the unopened crates to the Felinians—chose to deliver the weapons to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] instead.

==Biology and appearance==
The Felinians were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Feline/Legends|felinoids]] with a physiology that enabled them to wield [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] weapons intended for [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="LL" />

==Society and culture==
Members of the [[Species/Legends|species]] inhabited the [[Tree/Legends|jungles]] of the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Zoma V]],<ref name="LL" /> in the [[Zoma system/Legends|Zoma system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Hunnoverrs sector/Legends|Hunnoverrs sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Although their technology was considered primitive by the standards of the greater [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], at least some Felinians could use advanced weaponry such as blasters. The Felinians bartered for such goods by trading precious [[Stone/Legends|stones]].<ref name="LL" />

==History==
The Felinians hailed from<ref name="Planetnamia" /> the [[Felin system/Legends|Felin system]], a part of the [[Spinward sector/Legends|Spinward sector]] in the Outer Rim Territories.<ref name="EAOC" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a [[Saurian (species)|Saurian]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] named [[Sithless Leethe]] obtained a shipment of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] munitions that he attempted to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggle]] to the Felinians residing on Zoma V. He stored the weapons in a [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] called the ''[[Limping Lady]]'' on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and placed them under guard of his [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], another Saurian named [[Slopper]]. Leethe then found a band of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to buy the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] from him; as part of the deal, they agreed to deliver the cargo unopened to the Felinians on Zoma V.<ref name="LL" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
Nevertheless, the spacers deviated from the plan and took the weapons instead to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Leethe discovered their duplicity and retaliated by taking out a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] for their capture.<ref name="LL" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Felinians are mentioned in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying adventure]] "[[Limping Lady (article)|Limping Lady]]," which was written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]] and published in ''[[Challenge 64]]'' in [[September]] [[1992]]. As [[Challenge (magazine)|''Challenge'']] magazine was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="LL" /> it is considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The narrative above assumes the player characters—the unnamed spacers—deliver the cargo to the Rebel Alliance, as this is the scenario that affords them the largest reward and is considered the correct path. Nevertheless, what they do with Sithless Leethe's cargo is up to them; players may also opt to visit Zoma V and deliver the weapons to the Felinians. In such a scenario, the [[Cat/Legends|catlike]] [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] give the Saurian Slopper 5,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in precious stones in exchange.<ref name="LL" /> In [[July]] [[1993]], [[Role Player Independent 8|''Role Player Independent'' 8]] released the article "[[The Felin]]" which featured a very similar [[Felin|alien]].<ref name="Felin">{{RolePlayerIndependentCite|8|The Felin}}</ref>
